segments.text: This is a toast notification because it pops up just like a toast. This is a classic design trick where we are hiding real world objects in plain sight. Let's break down some of the coolest examples.
First up, radio buttons. You can only select one option at a time. That's because on old car radios when you press the button the other buttons would pop
out, so you could only choose one station. And this is a carousel. It is designed to show you a series of items
one after another just like a real life merrygoround where the horses and the content circle around for you to see.
Next one comes from food and you must have seen this everywhere lately. This is the bento grid. The design is directly inspired from the Japanese
bento lunch boxes which use compartments to keep food separate. So simple and smart. But my favorite is the next one
breadcrumbs. These little links show you the path you took on a website. And this comes from the story of Hansel and Gretle. They left breadcrumbs in the
forest so they could find their way home. And this is known as a subscribe button. You know what to do.
